The current best (0.9349-0.9350) uses the secondary-nucleation family with `c*(c+lag)` amplification and an inhibitor denominator. The dominant remaining error is shape loss (0.101 vs nmse 0.059). Let me look at whether the shape term (slope/timing) can be improved via a better onset structure.

The prompt notes solution length exceeds 500 chars — I should also aim to keep it compact. Let me make a targeted structural improvement rather than broadly explore, given we're only at iteration 7 and the family is strong.

Key insight: shape loss measures slope profile and half-response/onset timing. The current model reduces to the uninhibited law at cd=0, good. The weakness is likely per-curve onset timing across the cd sweep. The seed-coupled onset (Program 3) gave essentially the same score. Let me try a physically motivated change: make the autocatalytic amplification saturate in monomer via a smooth Michaelis-type term so high-monomer curves don't over-accelerate, which sharpens timing without over-parameterizing.

Let me do one quick targeted research check on secondary nucleation saturation form, then finalize.
